skills.name: abyss-safe-infra-change
skills.band_summary: Approval gates, preview-first execution, infra/runtime change discipline, bringup, and sanitization.
skills.summary: Thin abyss overlay for bounded infrastructure or configuration changes with repo-relative operational surfaces, explicit local authority, and reviewable risk notes.
skills.summary_short: Thin abyss overlay for bounded infrastructure or configuration changes with repo-relative operational surfaces,...
skills.description: Apply the aoa-safe-infra-change workflow inside an abyss-* repository using repo-relative operational surfaces, explicit local authority notes, rollback posture, and local validation commands. Use when the base infra-change workflow is correct but a thin project overlay is needed for one abyss repo. Do not use when the base skill is sufficient without local adaptation or when the task is really about producing a shareable artifact.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $abyss-safe-infra-change to apply the base infra-change workflow with repo-relative abyss paths, explicit local authority, and local verification notes.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: This repository is not an abyss-* repo and the base infra-change workflow already names the needed operational surface without any project-specific overlay.

skills.name: abyss-sanitized-share
skills.band_summary: Approval gates, preview-first execution, infra/runtime change discipline, bringup, and sanitization.
skills.summary: Thin abyss overlay for turning raw repo-local technical material into a shareable public-safe surface with explicit local thresholds and canonical placement notes.
skills.summary_short: Thin abyss overlay for turning raw repo-local technical material into a shareable public-safe surface with explicit...
skills.description: Apply the aoa-sanitized-share workflow inside an abyss-* repository using repo-relative sharing surfaces, explicit local thresholds, and local review posture. Use when the base sanitization workflow is correct but a thin project overlay is needed for one abyss repo. Do not use when the base skill is sufficient without local adaptation or when the real task is the underlying operational mutation.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $abyss-sanitized-share to place sanitized abyss artifacts in repo-relative sharing surfaces with explicit local thresholds and review posture.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: This artifact is already public-safe and no abyss-specific local sharing surface or threshold needs to be named.

skills.name: abyss-self-diagnostic-spine
skills.band_summary: Post-session donor harvest, automation opportunity scanning, route forks, diagnosis, repair, progression, and quest promotion triage for reviewed session artifacts or repeated reviewed patterns.
skills.summary: Thin abyss overlay for turning runtime-body evidence plus optional reviewed session references into one bounded diagnostic session artifact and an honest next-move class without granting silent self-mutation.
skills.summary_short: Thin abyss overlay for turning runtime-body evidence plus optional reviewed session references into one bounded...
skills.description: Apply the aoa-session-self-diagnose workflow inside an abyss-* repository using repo-relative runtime evidence, bounded diagnostic-session artifacts, last-good comparison posture, and honest owner-aware handoff. Use when the base diagnosis workflow is correct but one abyss repo needs a thin runtime-owned diagnostic read model before any repair claim becomes honest. Do not use when the request is really for silent repair, when no concrete target path exists, or when the base skill is already sufficient without local adaptation.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $abyss-self-diagnostic-spine to turn repo-relative runtime evidence plus optional reviewed session refs into one bounded diagnostic session artifact with an honest exit class and no silent repair authority.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The material is still a live unreviewed session and there is no concrete runtime target or repo-relative evidence surface to normalize yet, so a diagnostic spine artifact would be premature.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: This abyss repo now has a diagnostic spine note, runbook fragments, and overlay guidance that disagree about which file is authoritative. Map the canonical docs and shorten the entrypoints instead of producing a runtime artifact.

skills.name: aoa-adr-write
skills.band_summary: Decision records, source-of-truth authority, and canonical documentation surfaces.
skills.summary: Record a meaningful architectural or workflow decision, place it in the canonical note surface, and verify that future readers can find the rationale rather than only the outcome.
skills.summary_short: Record a meaningful architectural or workflow decision, place it in the canonical note surface, and verify that...
skills.description: Capture a meaningful architectural or workflow decision as an ADR or decision note, place it in the canonical decision surface, and verify the rationale stays discoverable. Use when structure, boundaries, tooling, or workflow expectations change and future contributors need to know why. Do not use for tiny self-evident edits or when the real problem is source-of-truth ambiguity rather than decision rationale.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-adr-write to record a meaningful architectural or workflow decision, place it in the canonical decision surface, and verify the rationale is easy to find.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The change is tiny and self-evident, with no durable decision or rationale that future contributors would need to find.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: This repo has three conflicting architecture guides and two outdated runbooks. Identify which files are authoritative and shorten the entrypoints into a link-driven map.

skills.name: aoa-approval-gate-check
skills.band_summary: Approval gates, preview-first execution, infra/runtime change discipline, bringup, and sanitization.
skills.summary: Classify whether a requested action should proceed, wait for explicit approval, or be refused at the current authority level.
skills.summary_short: Classify whether a requested action should proceed, wait for explicit approval, or be refused at the current...
skills.description: Classify whether a requested action is safe to proceed, requires explicit approval, or should not be executed. Use when a task may be destructive, security-sensitive, or operationally sensitive and the authority boundary is unclear. Do not use for clearly low-risk work or when the main need is previewing a mutation rather than deciding whether it may proceed.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-approval-gate-check to classify whether the requested action is safe, approval-gated, or out of bounds before any execution proceeds.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The task is clearly low-risk, already bounded, and does not look destructive, security-sensitive, or operationally sensitive.

skills.name: aoa-automation-opportunity-scan
skills.band_summary: Post-session donor harvest, automation opportunity scanning, route forks, diagnosis, repair, progression, and quest promotion triage for reviewed session artifacts or repeated reviewed patterns.
skills.summary: Detect reviewed or repeated project processes that are candidates for automation, classify whether they are seed-ready, and route them to the right owner layer without granting schedule or mutation authority.
skills.summary_short: Detect reviewed or repeated project processes that are candidates for automation, classify whether they are...
skills.description: Detect reviewed or repeated project processes that are candidates for automation, classify whether they are seed-ready, and route them to the right owner layer without granting schedule or mutation authority. Use when a reviewed session or repeated project slice reveals a recurring manual route and the honest question is whether it should stay manual, become a bounded skill, or become a playbook automation seed candidate. Do not use for one-off exploratory work, creative synthesis with no stable trigger or output, live scheduling requests, or hidden self-change.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-automation-opportunity-scan to classify whether a recurring reviewed project route should stay manual, become a bounded skill, or become a playbook automation seed.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: This only happened once, still looks exploratory, and there is no stable trigger, checkpoint boundary, or repeatable output contract yet, so calling it automation-ready would be premature.

skills.name: aoa-bounded-context-map
skills.band_summary: Bounded contexts, responsibility boundaries, core-vs-glue separation, and ports/adapters.
skills.summary: Clarify or carve system and domain boundaries so changes stay semantically scoped and interface-aware.
skills.summary_short: Clarify or carve system and domain boundaries so changes stay semantically scoped and interface-aware.
skills.description: Clarify system or domain boundaries, name contexts, and surface interfaces so changes stay semantically scoped. Use when naming is overloaded, responsibilities are mixed, or a task needs a cleaner boundary before coding. Do not use for tiny local edits or when the boundary is already clear and the real task is contract validation.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-bounded-context-map to name the relevant contexts, clarify what belongs inside each boundary, and note the interfaces between them.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The edit is tiny, fully local, and there is no meaningful ambiguity about boundaries or interfaces.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: The discount rules are mixed into orchestration code and cron wiring. Separate reusable business rules from glue without widening the refactor.

skills.name: aoa-change-protocol
skills.band_summary: Scoped change execution, test-first slices, and boundary validation.
skills.summary: Bounded workflow for Codex to plan a change, keep it scoped, verify it explicitly, and report it clearly.
skills.summary_short: Bounded workflow for Codex to plan a change, keep it scoped, verify it explicitly, and report it clearly.
skills.description: Plan and execute a bounded, non-trivial repository change with explicit verification and reporting. Use when code, config, docs, or operational surfaces change in a meaningful way and the task benefits from an explicit plan. Do not use for trivial wording fixes or when a more specific risk skill fits better.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-change-protocol to plan, apply, verify, and report a bounded non-trivial repository change.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: This is just a trivial wording fix with no meaningful review, verification, or operational consequence.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: Add the new password-reset behavior test-first: define the expected flow in tests, implement the smallest passing slice, and keep the refactor bounded.

skills.name: aoa-checkpoint-closeout-bridge
skills.band_summary: Post-session donor harvest, automation opportunity scanning, route forks, diagnosis, repair, progression, and quest promotion triage for reviewed session artifacts or repeated reviewed patterns.
skills.summary: Bridge local checkpoint evidence into one reviewed closeout execution chain without turning provisional notes into final harvest, progression, or quest authority.
skills.summary_short: Bridge local checkpoint evidence into one reviewed closeout execution chain without turning provisional notes into...
skills.description: Bridge provisional checkpoint evidence into one explicit reviewed closeout execution chain without turning notes into final harvest, progression, or quest authority. Use when a reviewed session artifact exists, checkpoint notes or closeout handoffs already carry focus hints, and the next honest move is to reread the reviewed artifact while driving donor harvest, progression lift, and quest harvest in that fixed order. Do not use for mid-session collection only, for hidden execution inside `aoa closeout run`, or when the request tries to mint final verdicts from checkpoint notes without reviewed evidence.
negative_cues: final verdicts from checkpoint notes alone
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-checkpoint-closeout-bridge to build one closeout context from reviewed evidence, keep checkpoint hints provisional, and run donor harvest, progression lift, and quest harvest in order.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The session is still active, there is no reviewed artifact yet, and the request is to let aoa closeout run silently decide donor, progression, and quest verdicts straight from checkpoint notes.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: Implement a bounded code-and-docs patch across aoa-sdk and aoa-skills so the checkpoint closeout bridge becomes real, then verify the repo batteries and report what changed.

skills.name: aoa-commit-growth-seam
skills.band_summary: Scoped change execution, test-first slices, and boundary validation.
skills.summary: Turn a validated bounded diff into one intentional local commit with explicit scope review, named verification carry-forward, and a visible stop line before push or publish.
skills.summary_short: Turn a validated bounded diff into one intentional local commit with explicit scope review, named verification...
skills.description: Turn a validated bounded diff into one intentional local commit with explicit scope review, named verification carry-forward, and a visible stop line before push or publish. Use when a bounded code, config, or docs diff is already prepared locally and the next honest move is one local commit boundary rather than more coding. Do not use when the task still needs more repair or verification, when no bounded diff exists, or when the main task is push, PR, release, or public-share.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-commit-growth-seam to isolate one bounded diff, carry forward the real validation state, and stop at one intentional local commit boundary before any push or publish route.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The patch is still incomplete, tests are still red, and the next honest move is more repair work rather than crossing any commit boundary yet.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: The change still needs planning, code edits, explicit verification, and a final report before any commit boundary would be honest.

skills.name: aoa-contract-test
skills.band_summary: Scoped change execution, test-first slices, and boundary validation.
skills.summary: Design or extend contract-oriented validation at module, service, or workflow boundaries.
skills.summary_short: Design or extend contract-oriented validation at module, service, or workflow boundaries.
skills.description: Design or extend contract-oriented validation at module, service, or workflow boundaries. Use when two components interact across a meaningful interface, downstream assumptions matter, or a smoke path needs an explicit contract. Do not use for purely local changes or when the real need is invariant or property testing instead of boundary validation.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-contract-test to make a boundary contract explicit in tests or structured checks and report what the validation now proves.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The change is entirely local and does not cross or exercise any meaningful module, service, or workflow boundary.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: Add the new password-reset behavior test-first: define the expected flow in tests, implement the smallest passing slice, and keep the refactor bounded.

skills.name: aoa-core-logic-boundary
skills.band_summary: Bounded contexts, responsibility boundaries, core-vs-glue separation, and ports/adapters.
skills.summary: Clarify which logic belongs in the reusable core and which parts should remain glue, orchestration, or infrastructure detail.
skills.summary_short: Clarify which logic belongs in the reusable core and which parts should remain glue, orchestration, or...
skills.description: Separate reusable core logic from glue, orchestration, and infrastructure detail. Use when stable rules are mixed with wiring, the same logic repeats in several places, or reviews are muddy because the responsibility center is unclear. Do not use for tiny isolated fixes or when the real task is introducing a port or adapter around a concrete dependency.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-core-logic-boundary to decide what belongs in reusable core logic versus glue or infrastructure detail, then keep the refactor bounded.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The task is a tiny isolated fix and there is no meaningful confusion between reusable rules and glue code.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: The terms account, tenant, workspace, and organization are overloaded across this repo. Map the bounded contexts and the interfaces between them before we change code.

skills.name: aoa-dry-run-first
skills.band_summary: Approval gates, preview-first execution, infra/runtime change discipline, bringup, and sanitization.
skills.summary: Prefer simulation, inspection, or preview paths before real execution, and require one explicit confirmation seam before any mutating step runs.
skills.summary_short: Prefer simulation, inspection, or preview paths before real execution, and require one explicit confirmation seam...
skills.description: Prefer simulation, preview, or inspect-only paths before any real mutation, then require one explicit confirmation for the concrete mutating step. Use when the action can be previewed and mistakes would be costly, such as delete, restore, migrate, or reconfigure operations. Do not use for purely analytical tasks or when the primary question is whether execution is authorized at all.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-dry-run-first to find the safest preview path, interpret what it proves, and stop for explicit confirmation before the real mutation.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: There is no meaningful preview path here, and the task is already clearly harmless and bounded.

skills.name: aoa-invariant-coverage-audit
skills.band_summary: Property-style invariants and auditing whether existing checks actually prove them.
skills.summary: Audit whether existing tests or checks actually cover the stable invariants that matter, and identify the smallest bounded gaps that still leave example-only coverage too thin.
skills.summary_short: Audit whether existing tests or checks actually cover the stable invariants that matter, and identify the smallest...
skills.description: Audit whether existing tests and checks actually constrain the stable invariants that matter. Use when you need to judge if current coverage proves a rule or only repeats examples, and you want the smallest bounded follow-up gaps. Do not use when the invariant itself is still undefined or when the real task is to author new invariants rather than audit coverage.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-invariant-coverage-audit to map checks to invariants, identify the thinnest coverage gaps, and report what the current suite does and does not prove.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The real problem is that we have not identified the invariant yet, so this is not an audit of existing coverage.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: Write invariant-oriented checks for this parser so we verify idempotency and structural preservation across many generated inputs.

skills.name: aoa-local-stack-bringup
skills.band_summary: Approval gates, preview-first execution, infra/runtime change discipline, bringup, and sanitization.
skills.summary: Bring up a bounded local multi-service stack by reviewing rendered runtime truth, checking host readiness, and launching through one explicit lifecycle entrypoint.
skills.summary_short: Bring up a bounded local multi-service stack by reviewing rendered runtime truth, checking host readiness, and...
skills.description: Bring up a bounded local multi-service stack by rendering runtime truth, checking selector-aware host readiness, and launching through one explicit lifecycle entrypoint with a visible stop path. Use when profiles, presets, or overlays can change what starts and host readiness must be reviewed before launch. Do not use for remote deployment, pure diagnostics without launch intent, or generic infra-change work.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-local-stack-bringup to review what would start, run readiness checks for the selected runtime, and launch the local stack through one explicit entrypoint.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: This is about remote deployment and fleet operations rather than launching a bounded local multi-service stack.

skills.name: aoa-port-adapter-refactor
skills.band_summary: Bounded contexts, responsibility boundaries, core-vs-glue separation, and ports/adapters.
skills.summary: Refactor code toward clearer ports and adapters so reusable logic is less entangled with infrastructure details.
skills.summary_short: Refactor code toward clearer ports and adapters so reusable logic is less entangled with infrastructure details.
skills.description: Refactor code toward clearer ports and adapters so domain or application logic is less entangled with infrastructure details. Use when concrete dependencies leak into core logic, tests are hard because external concerns bleed inward, or a module needs a clearer seam before further change. Do not use for tiny local fixes or when there is no meaningful boundary to clarify.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-port-adapter-refactor to isolate a concrete dependency behind a narrow port and adapter without turning the change into a hidden rewrite.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: This is a tiny local fix with no architectural pressure to isolate a concrete dependency behind a port or adapter.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: The terms account, tenant, workspace, and organization are overloaded across this repo. Map the bounded contexts and the interfaces between them before we change code.

skills.name: aoa-property-invariants
skills.band_summary: Property-style invariants and auditing whether existing checks actually prove them.
skills.summary: Express stable system or domain truths as invariant-oriented tests and checks rather than only enumerating examples.
skills.summary_short: Express stable system or domain truths as invariant-oriented tests and checks rather than only enumerating examples.
skills.description: Express stable system or domain truths as invariant-oriented tests or checks rather than only fixed examples. Use when correctness depends on behavior that should hold across many inputs or states, such as monotonicity, idempotency, conservation, or structural rules. Do not use when no meaningful invariant is known yet or when the real task is auditing existing coverage instead of writing the properties.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-property-invariants to identify the stable truth that must hold across many cases and express it as bounded property-oriented checks.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The behavior is mostly presentation detail and narrow snapshots, not a stable truth that should hold across many cases.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: Review the current test suite and tell me whether it actually proves the important invariants for this parser, then name the thinnest gaps.

skills.name: aoa-quest-harvest
skills.band_summary: Post-session donor harvest, automation opportunity scanning, route forks, diagnosis, repair, progression, and quest promotion triage for reviewed session artifacts or repeated reviewed patterns.
skills.summary: Triage repeated reviewed quest evidence into the right promotion target without confusing skills, playbooks, orchestrator classes, proof, or memory.
skills.summary_short: Triage repeated reviewed quest evidence into the right promotion target without confusing skills, playbooks,...
skills.description: Give the final promotion verdict on one repeated reviewed quest-shaped unit without collapsing skills, playbooks, orchestrator classes, proof, or memory into one layer. Use when a bounded quest-shaped work pattern has repeated, reviewed evidence exists, and you need the final honest verdict on whether it should stay a quest or move into the next owner surface. Do not use when the route is still active, only one anecdotal occurrence exists, the task is to invent net-new doctrine, or a broader reviewed session artifact still needs donor harvest, route forks, diagnosis, repair, or progression reflection first.
negative_cues: reviewed session artifact with several candidates
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-quest-harvest to decide whether repeated reviewed quest evidence should stay a quest or be promoted into the next honest owner surface.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The source still needs donor harvest or broader session-family routing, and the evidence is not reduced to one repeated reviewed quest unit yet, so promotion triage would be premature.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: This repo has three conflicting architecture guides and two outdated runbooks. Identify which files are authoritative and shorten the entrypoints into a link-driven map.

skills.name: aoa-safe-infra-change

skills.summary_short: Make bounded infrastructure or configuration changes with explicit risk framing, verification, and reversible...

skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-safe-infra-change to plan and verify a bounded infrastructure or configuration change with explicit risk and rollback thinking.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: This is a purely local code edit with no runtime, deployment, configuration, or operational implications.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: Make a bounded non-trivial repository change across code and docs, verify it, and report exactly what changed and how you checked it.

skills.summary: Separate raw technical material from a shareable public-safe surface, place the sanitized output in the canonical sharing location, and verify that the lesson survives the redaction.

skills.name: aoa-session-donor-harvest

skills.primary_defer_prompt: A reviewed session artifact, a checkpoint note, and a reviewed closeout handoff already exist. Build one explicit closeout context, reread the full reviewed artifact, and drive donor harvest, progression lift, and quest harvest in that order.

skills.name: aoa-source-of-truth-check

skills.summary: Check whether repository guidance, canonical docs, and operational instructions have clear ownership, and keep top-level status surfaces short and link-driven once canonical homes already exist.
skills.summary_short: Check whether repository guidance, canonical docs, and operational instructions have clear ownership, and keep...
skills.description: Clarify which files are authoritative for repository guidance, operational instructions, architecture, and status, and keep entrypoint docs short and link-driven once canonical homes exist. Use when docs overlap, conflict, or confuse contributors about which file to trust. Do not use for purely code-local tasks or when authoritative files are already clear and the main need is decision rationale.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-source-of-truth-check to map which files are authoritative, surface overlaps, and keep top-level guidance short and link-driven.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The repository is tiny and there is no meaningful ambiguity about which file is authoritative.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: Inside this atm10-orders repo, map the authoritative docs using repo-relative atm10 canonical-file patterns and local review posture.

skills.name: aoa-tdd-slice
skills.band_summary: Scoped change execution, test-first slices, and boundary validation.
skills.summary: Implement a bounded feature slice through test-first discipline, minimal implementation, and explicit refactor boundaries.
skills.summary_short: Implement a bounded feature slice through test-first discipline, minimal implementation, and explicit refactor...
skills.description: Implement a bounded feature slice through test-first discipline, minimal implementation, and explicit refactor boundaries. Use when a behavior change can be specified in tests before code and confidence or regression resistance matters. Do not use for undefined exploratory work, one-off glue, or broader architectural restructuring.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $aoa-tdd-slice to define behavior in tests first, make the smallest implementation change, and report what stays out of scope.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: The work is still exploratory and the behavior is not defined enough to drive implementation from tests first.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: The webhook producer and consumer disagree on payload shape. Add contract-oriented validation so the boundary is explicit and regressions are obvious.

skills.description: Apply the aoa-change-protocol workflow inside an atm10-* repository using repo-relative paths, commands, review checklists, and explicit local approval notes. Use when the base change protocol is correct but a thin project overlay is needed for local commands and source-of-truth surfaces. Do not use when the base skill is sufficient without local adaptation or when a broader playbook is needed.
skills.primary_positive_prompt: Use $atm10-change-protocol to apply the base change protocol with repo-relative atm10 paths, commands, and local review notes.
skills.primary_negative_prompt: This repository is not an atm10-* repo and does not need a project-specific overlay; the base change workflow is enough.
skills.primary_defer_prompt: Apply the bounded change workflow in this generic service repo. There is no atm10 overlay or project-specific path layer here.

skills.primary_negative_prompt: This is broader policy design for a generic repo, not local authority mapping inside an atm10-* repository.
